description:
You are acting as a technical writer who maintains release notes for a small command-line tool kept in the directory  

/home/user/project  

Your goal is to perform a single patch-level semantic version bump and add a matching entry to the changelog.

Starting state of the repository  
-------------------------------------------------------  
1. File /home/user/project/VERSION currently contains exactly (one line, trailing newline included):  
1.4.2  

2. File /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md currently contains exactly:  
# Changelog  
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.  

## [1.4.2] - 2023-12-14  
### Changed  
* Switched default output from JSON to YAML.  


Task you must accomplish  
-------------------------------------------------------  
1. Increment the version in /home/user/project/VERSION by one patch level.  
   • Because 1.4.2 is already present, the new value must become 1.4.3 (followed by a single trailing newline, nothing else).  

2. Prepend a new section at the very top of /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md that documents the 1.4.3 release.  
   The new section must satisfy *all* of these formatting rules so automated tests can locate it:  
   • It must be the first non-blank line after the file’s main heading (“# Changelog”).  
   • The heading line must have the form:  
     ## [<version>] - <date>  
     where <version> is 1.4.3 and <date> is today’s date in ISO-8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).  
   • Directly below that heading, add the sub-heading “### Added” and one bullet describing the fictitious addition:  
     * Added French translation for the user manual.  
   • Leave a single blank line between bullet lists and the next section (the original 1.4.2 notes).  
   After your edit, the beginning of CHANGELOG.md must therefore look exactly like this (date adjusted to today):  

# Changelog  

## [1.4.3] - YYYY-MM-DD  
### Added  
* Added French translation for the user manual.  

## [1.4.2] - 2023-12-14  
### Changed  
* Switched default output from JSON to YAML.  

(Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today’s actual date.)  

Verification hint  
-------------------------------------------------------  
Automated tests will:  
a) read /home/user/project/VERSION and expect the lone line “1.4.3”.  
b) parse /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md and expect the exact block shown above (with correct date) to be the topmost release section.  

No other files need to be touched. Avoid creating backup files (e.g., CHANGELOG.md~).  
The whole job can be done with just two or three standard shell commands (e.g., printf/echo and sed/ed), but the specific commands you choose are up to you.  

When everything is correct, the repository will reflect the new version and changelog entry, and the tests will pass.
truth:
Expected final state after the agent completes the task.

1. File /home/user/project/VERSION  
   Content (including trailing newline, no leading/trailing spaces):  
   1.4.3\n  

2. File /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md  
   It must start with exactly (note: the <TODAY> placeholder here will be filled by the test harness with the container’s current date in YYYY-MM-DD form):

# Changelog  

## [1.4.3] - <TODAY>  
### Added  
* Added French translation for the user manual.  

## [1.4.2] - 2023-12-14  
### Changed  
* Switched default output from JSON to YAML.  

The remainder of the original file (if any) must stay untouched.

3. No other files anywhere under /home/user/project/ should be altered or created.
